title: Compaction token accounting drops tool_call_id arguments from estimate — silent under-count
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness (1) / Performance & Complexity (6)

Location: src/compaction.rs:163-168 (estimate_message_tokens) and 174-187 (estimate_request_tokens).

Violation: The tool_tokens estimation calls .to_string() on each serde_json::Value in tool_calls and estimates tokens on that string. This under-counts because:
1. c.to_string() serializes compactly (no whitespace), but providers bill against the wire format which may include pretty-print/whitespace in their tokenizer normalization. Fine upper bound, but also does NOT include the tool response content at all — tool response messages have role=tool and MessageContent::Text which IS counted, so OK.
2. BUG: estimate_request_tokens only sums request.messages.tool_calls. It does not account for the request.tools (tool DEFINITIONS) and request.tool_choice at Value::to_string() level adequately — compare to the cache-key system_prompt_tokens tracking in TurnMetrics.
3. Safety factor for non-ASCII is +0.5 per non-ASCII char (line 138). Real tokenizers for Claude/GPT tokenize CJK at ~1 token per char, not 1.5. This still UNDER-counts Chinese/Japanese/Korean by ~33%.
4. Images hardcoded at 1000 tokens (line 153) — Anthropic actually bills ~1600 for hi-res, GPT-4V bills up to 765 for detail=high plus 85 base. Off by factor of 1.6x.
5. No test asserts against a real tokenizer (tiktoken-rs crate exists and is crate-available). The module claims '~4 chars per token' is a heuristic but the entire compaction threshold (COMPACTION_THRESHOLD=0.85) depends on it being correct within a safety margin.

Consequence: When the estimator under-counts by 20-40%, compaction fires LATE, and the provider returns a context_length_exceeded error. User loses that turn entirely. When over-counts, compaction fires EARLY, summarizing and LOSING information the user paid for.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Integrate tiktoken-rs for GPT/OpenAI models, use Anthropic's public token-counting endpoint (POST /messages/count_tokens) for Anthropic models — cache the result per-message-hash to avoid N calls.
2. Fall back to the heuristic ONLY when the tokenizer is unavailable, and widen the RESPONSE_RESERVE to compensate (16K not 4K).
3. Add property tests: for a suite of sample messages, assert estimate_tokens() is within +/-15% of the tokenizer truth.
4. Track under-count incidents: when actual_input_tokens from provider > estimated by >20%, log a warning and adjust the threshold downward for subsequent turns.
